prerequisites: A Xero account with access to one or more Xero organisations (tenants) whose accounting data you want to sync.
A Xero app created in the Xero Developer portal at https://developer.xero.com/myapps, which provides the OAuth 2.0 Client ID and Client Secret.
A redirect URI configured on the Xero app that matches the one used during the OAuth 2.0 authorization flow.

OAuth 2.0 credentials populating the connector's `credentials` config (client_id, client_secret, and an access_token obtained via the authorization code flow), with the `offline_access` scope authorized so a refresh token can be issued.
The `tenant_id` (Xero-tenant-id) of the target Xero organisation, obtained by calling the Xero `/connections` endpoint with a valid access token.
A `start_date` to bound the earliest records the connector retrieves.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 (authorization code flow)
authMethods.summary: Xero authenticates via OAuth 2.0. Create an app in the Xero Developer portal to obtain a Client ID and Client Secret (`credentials.client_secret`), then run the authorization code flow to obtain an access token (`credentials.access_token`). Request the `offline_access` scope so a refresh token is issued, since access tokens are short-lived. Authorize the data scopes (for example accounting scopes) needed by the streams. The granted organisation is targeted through its `tenant_id`, which is returned by Xero's `/connections` endpoint.

setupSteps.title: Create a Xero app and get OAuth credentials
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Xero Developer portal at https://developer.xero.com/myapps and create an app. Xero assigns a unique Client ID and lets you generate a Client Secret (keep the secret private). Configure a redirect URI on the app; it must exactly match the redirect URI you use in the authorization flow.

setupSteps.title: Authorize and obtain an access token
setupSteps.body: Run the OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow against Xero. Include the `offline_access` scope (required to receive a refresh token) plus the data scopes your streams need, and exchange the returned authorization code at the token endpoint (https://identity.xero.com/connect/token) for an access token and refresh token. Access tokens are short-lived, so the refresh token is used to obtain new ones.

setupSteps.title: Identify the Xero tenant (organisation)
setupSteps.body: Call Xero's `/connections` endpoint with your access token to list the organisations the user consented to. Each connected organisation has a unique tenant ID. Use the target organisation's tenant ID for the connector's `tenant_id` field.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Populate the `credentials` config with the Client ID, Client Secret (`credentials.client_secret`), and access token (`credentials.access_token`). Set `tenant_id` to the target organisation's tenant ID, and set `start_date` to the earliest date from which records should be retrieved.
